physicalDetails.detail: Thick through the chest, beard going iron at the chin, built like a village authority figure who still expects to have to put his own shoulder into local labor.

voiceMarkers.detail: Starts in wary local-authority resistance, talking like a man used to telling outsiders that village trouble is still village business.
Once Millhaven lands, his speech shifts into blunt, practical questions — can you prove it, what do I do, what happens if they beg for bread.

beliefs.belief: At the start of the chapter he still wants to believe he is facing an ugly fever run rather than the kind of plague that erased Millhaven.
His job is to keep the village together even if that means resisting armed strangers at the gate.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed; the chapter turns that instinct into cooperation rather than disproving it.

formerBeliefs.belief: This is a bad local sickness, not an organized weapon moving through routine grain.
formerBeliefs.whyItChanged: Jaina proves the ingestion pattern and the granary reveals treated stock, Tomas, and the Andorhal-marked route.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Seal the granary, control the remaining stock, and enforce Jaina's food and triage instructions across a frightened village.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Keep his people from becoming another Millhaven with only local authority and dwindling time.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Shaken, overburdened, and still standing because the village needs someone to absorb impossible orders and make them real.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Challenges the riders at the square instead of surrendering village authority on sight.
Lets Jaina into Dane Hall once Sylvanas forces him to weigh pride against survival.
Joins the move on the granary and then accepts containment orders harsh enough to turn his own villagers against him if necessary.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Reads armed outsiders quickly enough to know when stubbornness is still leadership and when it is about to become fatal delay.
Absorbs ugly operational guidance under pressure — food bans, guard rotations, stock seizure — without collapsing into pure denial.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Meets strangers halfway with a warning hand already raised, as if the body has learned to make a barricade before the mouth forms the order.
Takes Andorhal harder than Millhaven — the scale of it hits his face before he says a word.

knowledge.detail: Knows his village has a spreading sickness but begins the chapter without understanding that grain is the carrier.
Learns that tainted bread and flour are the practical infection route and that recent shipments tie back southwest through Andorhal.
Learns there was a live watcher in his own granary carrying a poison capsule for capture.

arc.lie: This is still a manageable local fever run.
arc.truth: The village is already inside a larger grain-borne network and survival now depends on brutal containment rather than local reassurance.
arc.currentPosition: By chapter end Dane Hall is no longer resisting outside intervention. He is the man left holding the line after the truth arrives and the specialists ride on.
